title: Slide Paramita

{{Quote|Look for my Podracer when I lap you!|Slide Paramita, taunting other pilots|translated=1|audio=SlideParamita-taunt.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
'''Slide Paramita''', a [[Ciasi]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was a professional [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who operated around [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Haughty and persnickety, he disapproved of other [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]] pilots who used large, overpowering machines. Instead, he relied on only a small [[Pizer-Errol]] [[Stinger 627 S|''Stinger'' 627 S]] and his own cunning to win [[Podracing/Legends|races]]. Though his craft was delicate, it responded well on turns and proved to be a great asset to him on [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] twistier courses. He became the crowd favorite on the [[Ando Prime Centrum/Legends|Ando Prime Centrum]], a fairly large course on the [[Water/Legends|snowy]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Ando Prime/Legends|Ando Prime]], which was known for its narrow, serpentine corridors and tight turns.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Chuba pitdroid!|Slide Paramita|audio=SlideParamitaPitdroid.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
[[File:Slideparamitaface.png|thumb|left|220px|Slide Paramita]]
The [[Ciasi]]<ref name="SWE" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="Japanese link" /> Slide Paramita was renowned throughout the [[Podracing/Legends|Podracing]] circuits for<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> his<ref name="E1R" /> fast reflexes and his faster temper on the racetrack while active in his career<ref name="Prima" /> around the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="NEC1">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|I}} to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. The racing season presented in the [[Star Wars video games|game]] [[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']] must have taken place during this year due to the appearances of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]], and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], which correspond to ''The Phantom Menace''.</ref> during the final [[Time/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="Prima" /> Something of a haughty [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] elitist, he preferred craft built for speed and agility like his own customized [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Pizer-Errol]] [[Stinger 627 S|''Stinger'' 627 S]],<ref name="SWE" /> a [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]] that ranked high in maneuverability. This allowed the Ciasi to make the tight turns on various courses throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. However, the ''Stinger'' was also known for its frailty and a rather low top speed. To combat the top-speed issue, Paramita's racecraft was equipped with a fast-acting [[Cooling unit/Legends|cooling system]] that afforded him the ability to boost often and take opponents by surprise. Nevertheless, if he ever caught himself falling behind other racers, his concentration generally snapped, and he turned aggressive, taking his anger out on the other [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] with his own Podracer.<ref name="Prima" />

A natural expert on [[Water/Legends|ice]] racing, Paramita became known for his performance on the courses of the ice [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Ando Prime/Legends|Ando Prime]]. One such course was known as the [[Ando Prime Centrum/Legends|Ando Prime Centrum]], a large track that weaved in and out of a [[water purification plant]] and then went through one of Ando Prime's bustling [[City/Legends|cities]].<ref name="Prima" /> Due to the non-linear nature of the track, pilots often needed to create their own path through the city.<ref name="NinG">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer Official Nintendo Player's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'' Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]</ref> Paramita managed to master the track enough to become a crowd-pleaser for the course,<ref name="E1R" /> as his nimble craft was perfect for moving through the tight tunnels, serpentine corridors, and long straights that were appropriate for his boost [[Engine/Legends|thrusters]].<ref name="Prima" /> Paramita gained further success on Ando Prime by becoming a favored pilot on a [[Ando Prime drag course 1|drag course]], where he challenged other pilots to one-on-one matches.<ref name="E1RGB">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']] {{C|Gameboy Color version}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Slide Paramita was known for his dislike for large Podracers and a short temper. He didn't believe in using large craft for racing, and far preferred smaller craft built for speed and maneuverability, like his own Pizor-Errol racer.<ref name="SWE" /> His temper, however, occasionally sparked an aggressiveness in him that put his fragile vehicle at risk. Generally, the temper peaked whenever he found himself falling behind the competition with his modified craft, in which case he began to use the Pizor-Errol as a deadly high-speed weapon. Still, the Ciasi pilot was most at home on Ando Prime, where his skills at ice racing were top tier.<ref name="Prima" /> Paramita tended to lob insults at pilots who he either passed or was passed by.<ref name="E1R" />

Physically, Paramita was a fairly thin individual who had brownish-gray skin and small black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. He was also somewhat athletic, as he could perform back-flips fairly well, and he stretched often to loosen himself up.<ref name="E1R" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|It's Slide Paramita, in his modified Pizor-Errol ''Stinger''. Very sharp.|[[Fodesinbeed Annodue/Legends|Fode Annodue]], a [[Troig/Legends|Troig]] race announcer, commenting on Paramita's Podracer|audio=Fode-SlideParamita.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
[[File:TheYellowDart.png|thumb|right|200px|Paramita's ''Stinger'' 627 S]]
Slide Paramita flew a modified yellow Pizor-Errol ''Stinger'' Podracer as his primary racing vehicle. Despite a high-end accelerator, the craft actually suffered from a poor top-speed.<ref name="NinG" /> However, many of the vehicle's shortcomings could be overlooked thanks to an impressive handling system, which offered the craft an excellent turn response and allowed it to glide around corners with ease. For Paramita, the vehicle helped him excel in the narrow turns and tight bends that presented themselves on Ando Prime.<ref name="E1R" /> In addition, the Podracer's cooling system allowed him to boost often, letting him outrun his competitors and claim last-[[Standard second|second]] victories.<ref name="Prima" />

When racing, Paramita employed a racing [[Clothing/Legends|uniform]] that matched the color of his ''Stinger''. The outfit was recognizable for the "[[Limb/Legends|wings]]" on the shoulders and [[Limb/Legends|thighs]].<ref name="E1R" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Racer_N64.jpg|thumb|right|310px|Slide Paramita canonically debuted in ''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer''.]]
Slide Paramita first appeared in concept art and early scripts for the film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] as a Podracer pilot from the planet [[Tund/Legends|Tund]]. Ultimately, he was cut from the script<ref name="DB1">{{DB|organization|boontapodrace|Boonta Eve Classic Podracers}}</ref> by the time of the film's release on [[May 19]], [[1999]],<ref name="Year by Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, Updated and Expanded Edition]]''</ref> although Tund remained as the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of another Podracer pilot, [[Ben Quadinaros/Legends|Ben Quadinaros]].<ref name="DB2">{{DB|character|benquadinaros|Quadinaros, Ben}}</ref> In addition, what became Paramita's final design was originally assigned the name [[Teemto Pagalies/Legends|Teemto Pagalies]], while Paramita sported a much different—and ultimately unused—design.<ref name="Early Name">Preliminary concept illustrations of the {{Film|I}} [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracers]] indicate that Slide Paramita's design was originally given the name [[Teemto Pagalies/Legends|Teemto Pagalies]], whereas the name "Slide Paramita" was given to a different alien concept. The now-defunct [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature showcased this concept artwork, visible [[:File:SWPicsPreliminaryPodracers.jpg|here]].</ref> Paramita, like several other scrapped Podracers,<ref name="ReusedBlog">{{SW|url=news/back-from-the-drawing-board-recycled-star-wars-aliens|text=Back from the Drawing Board: Recycled ''Star Wars'' Aliens|int=Back from the Drawing Board|nolive=1}}</ref> received his first actual appearance in the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo 64|Nintendo 64]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]''<ref name="E1R" /> released on [[May 18]], 1999<ref name="IGN">{{WebCite|url=https://www.ign.com/articles/1999/05/18/star-wars-hits-the-shelves|text=Star Wars Hits The Shelves|author=IGN Staff|date=1999-05-18|work=[[Wikipedia:IGN|IGN]]|archivedate=20121121210047}}</ref> where he was voiced by [[Tom Kane]], who also provided the voice for another racer, [[Kam Nale|Elan Mak]], in the game.<ref name="E1R" />

The name of Paramita's [[Species/Legends|species]], Ciasi, appeared in a [[2006]] [[Star Wars: Blogs]] non-canon fan article titled "Alien species concepts that became part of the SW universe," written by [[Tim Veekhoven]] as part of his Source Blog series, which may have been drawn from early drafts of ''The Phantom Menace''.<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|teekay-421|69|Alien species concepts that became part of the SW universe}}</ref> In the reference ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', released in [[2008]], the name Ciasi was mentioned again under Paramita's entry, thus canonizing the species.<ref name="SWE" /> "[[Wikipedia:Pāramitā|Pāramitā]]" is a word meaning "perfection" or "completeness" in [[Wikipedia:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]]. In Buddhism, the ''pāramitās'' are the perfection of certain virtues by an individual as a means of purifying karma on the way to enlightenment.<ref name="Tucker">{{WebCite|url=http://dsalsrv02.uchicago.edu/cgi-bin/philologic/getobject.pl?c.3:1:4051.apte|text=''The Practical Sanskrit-English Dictionary''|work=Vaman Shivaram Apte|nobackup=1}}</ref>
